title: Questions about the ending of Re Take
display_title: Questions about the ending of Re Take
wikitext:
[]: Questions about the ending of Re-Take             Quote Post by glitz2hard » Sat Jul 11, 2020 9:24 am For reference, I read the All-Ages version. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Questions about the ending of Re-Take             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Jul 11, 2020 9:46 am I'm more or less as lost as you are when I read it way back when; I'm sure there is some degree of thought put into it, but the way I see it, it's just half-assed shipping bullshit with a good dose of pretentious and overly self-indulgent "oh but I am so deep" slapped on top of it when the author realized that the natural story progression conflicted with his OTP. Just for the record, the nsfw version is exactly the same, to my knowledge all that's cut in the all ages version is a sex scene that's more misery porn than actual porn. But yeah, I'd say don't put too much thought to it, retake really isn't worth the attention of doing that

[]: Re: Questions about the ending of Re-Take             Quote Post by glitz2hard » Sat Jul 11, 2020 4:50 pm Blockio wrote: I'm more or less as lost as you are when I read it way back when; I'm sure there is some degree of thought put into it, but the way I see it, it's just half-assed shipping bullshit with a good dose of pretentious and overly self-indulgent "oh but I am so deep" slapped on top of it when the author realized that the natural story progression conflicted with his OTP. Just for the record, the nsfw version is exactly the same, to my knowledge all that's cut in the all ages version is a sex scene that's more misery porn than actual porn. But yeah, I'd say don't put too much thought to it, retake really isn't worth the attention of doing that

[]: Re: Questions about the ending of Re-Take             Quote Post by Derantor » Sat Jul 11, 2020 6:44 pm *Rushing in before the closure happens* It's really not that hard. Shinji "dies" in the sense that he gives up on trying to create one dream-world after another, trying to go after his "perfect" Asuka. He gives up his role as "god". Having now accepted reality, he wakes up with the true Asuka at the beach of EoE, they intertwine fingers, future unclear, The End. Basically just a retread of EoE, slightly less miserable. Not sure which little girl you're talking about. There's a personification of Asuka talking to her, and the one showing up at the beach in the end. No clue who the second one is, and it isn't important. She's just there to discover the two pilots. And really, as far as shipping bullshit goes, Re-Take is on the lower end of the scale, imho. The truly horrific waffy stuff is all a dream anyways. Not sure why people are so utterly repulsed by it.

Article: Questions about the ending of Re Take
2020 6:44 pm *Rushing in before the closure happens* It's really not that hard. Shinji "dies" in the sense that he gives up on trying to create one dream-world after another, trying to go after his "perfect" Asuka. He gives up his role as "god". Having now accepted reality, he wakes up with the true Asuka at the beach of EoE, they intertwine fingers, future unclear, The End. Basically just a retread of EoE, slightly less miserable. Not sure which little girl you're talking about. There's a personification of Asuka talking to her, and the one showing up at the beach in the end. No clue who the second one is, and it isn't important. She's just there to discover the two pilots. And really, as far as shipping bullshit goes, Re-Take is on the lower end of the scale, imho. The truly horrific waffy stuff is all a dream anyways. Not sure why people are so utterly repulsed by it.
